title: tengine.diag.wt_telemetry v1
description: Per-WT GPU cost snapshot emitted by WtTelemetryDrainer every ~30 rendered frames. When profiling injection is active (TENGINE_PROFILE_ALL_WTS=1 or ENABLE_SHADER_PROFILING=1), uses the cumulative WorkTypeTelemetry ring (RES_WORK_TYPE_TELEMETRY, slot 113) as its source — the same data that work_type_timing.json uses. The amortization_basis field tells consumers which denominator was used: 'silo_frames' means the 25x gotcha may apply for activation:once WTs.

frame.description: GPU frame index at which this window was flushed

window_frames.description: number of silo frames covered by this window; equals silo_frames for the cumulative path

wt_id.description: base work type ID (TSDL wt_id, not telemetry slot)

name.description: work type name from the TSDL manifest (profiler label)

activation.description: declared activation kind from TSDL. 'on_dirty' with high fire_frames is a scheduling smell. 'unknown' is the fallback when the TSDL token is unrecognised.

delta_cycles.description: GPU clock cycles accumulated over the window; when amortization_basis='silo_frames' this is the cumulative total since silo start

fire_frames.description: number of frames in which this WT ran; equals window_frames for the cumulative path

est_ms_per_frame.description: estimated GPU cost per frame in milliseconds; honest when amortization_basis='wt_fire_frames', potentially 25x underestimate when 'silo_frames' for activation:once WTs

amortization_basis.description: denominator used for est_ms_per_frame: 'wt_fire_frames'=honest per-dispatch cost; 'silo_frames'=amortized over all frames (may understate activation:once WTs)
